quote_of_day.text: If pajeet leaders spent half the energy on building factories, manufacturing, and actual R&D instead of non-stop bhangi reservation scams, caste votebank warfare and "hisab chukta" politics, India wouldn't be a laughingstock still importing everything today.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of anxiety over 'Chaddi' inundation persisted through the morning, with one anon describing a fear of their uninhibited presence. This unease brought scattered showers of dread.

A cold front of criticism moved in, bringing biting remarks about sacred animals in Hinduism. Anon dismissed the notion of revering certain creatures, calling it 'retarded.' This blast of skepticism was brief but sharp.

A high-pressure ridge of discontent with India's leadership and development stretched across the board, with criticism focusing on misplaced priorities and ineffective governance.

Meanwhile, a scattered drizzle of inquiries and pleas for information touched on the disappearance of 'bharatchan' and the board's ownership. A rogue call for British colonization of India emerged, marked by intense dislike for the current state of affairs.

articles.title: Anon Poster Describes Fear of 'Chaddi' Inundation
articles.body:
Anon expressed fear of being overwhelmed by 'Chaddi' immigrants, describing them as having "0 impulse control like a homo erectus." Anon responded with various claims about the supposed shortcomings of certain groups, including the assertion that "nigger leaders are often incompetent af" and that they are "corrupt" and prone to making "dumb decisions." Another anon described fantasies involving Candace Owens, suggesting that she would look good cosplaying a Dalit girl and chanting "Jai Bhim slogans." Anon also discussed the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, with one poster noting that Israel had "raped nations for less" and another suggesting that Israel's actions in Uganda were an example of their military prowess.

The conversation also touched on the topic of race and intelligence, with one anon stating that "even a little contamination with negro blood can bring down the mean iq of a race" and another suggesting that certain groups were "not human" and should be treated as such.

Anon debated the capabilities of various nations, with one poster arguing that India is "objectively far better than subsaharan africa" due to its economic and technological advancements.

The discussion also involved fantasies about violence and domination, including one poster's claim that they would have done the same as Hitler if they were in his position.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Sacred Animals in Hinduism
articles.body:
Anon, who called themselves 'Savarkar-pilled', started the discussion by criticizing the concept of sacred animals in Hinduism, specifically cows.

They argued that having a sacred animal is 'the most retarded thing a culture could do' and that eating beef is 'based' and just a matter of consuming an animal.

Anon responded by comparing dietary restrictions across religions, stating that while Muslims avoid pork due to a sense of disgust, Hindus consider cows sacred.

Another anon countered that the issue with sacred animals is that it allows outsiders to mock and feel superior to Hindus.

They also discussed the origins of certain religious practices, such as circumcision, and how they may have been adopted or discontinued across different cultures.

Anon also brought up the point that Muslims are offended by actions that directly target their beliefs, such as throwing pork at a mosque or posting images of it with the Quran.

They contrasted this with Hinduism, where, according to one anon, most Hindus, even religious ones, do not care what non-Hindus do in non-Hindu lands.

The discussion also touched on the natural behavior of pigs and the environments in which they are raised. One anon pointed out that pigs are clean animals in their natural environments, with separate areas for sleeping and defecating.

Others debated the merits of dietary prohibitions and their origins, with some attributing them to superstition or cultural practices.

One anon proposed creating a new sect of Hinduism that is more accepting of meat-eating and free from what they termed 'religious retardation'.

They suggested that this new sect could be inspired by the Indus Valley Civilization, which they claimed had a diet rich in various meats, including beef, pork, and seafood.

articles.title: Criticism of India's Leadership and Development
articles.body:
Anon criticized India's leaders, saying they spend too much energy on scams, caste votebank warfare, and "hisab chukta" politics, rather than building factories, manufacturing, and R&D. This, according to Anon, is why India remains a laughingstock, still importing everything.

Another anon responded, suggesting that if India's leaders disappeared overnight, not much would change.

This anon argued that even if India paused its current issues and dedicated resources to solving one problem, like cleaning the Yamuna river, it could make significant progress.

Anon also discussed the military, stating that modernizing and expanding it to a point where it can sustain offensive operations in neighboring countries would require significant investment, around $100 billion.

This anon believed that with such investment, India could have a capable military. Other anons shared their frustration with India's development.

One anon mentioned the need to work on sewage management and rivers, stating that the current direction is leading to an "urban heat trap" infested with the Dalit population.

Another anon expressed concern that the government is not taking care of the population. Anon also brought up the idea that the Indian government might be infiltrated and backed by the CIA to keep the country unstable.

This anon noted that things have only gotten worse over time, and it's possible that the government is not working in the country's best interests. The discussion also touched on the military's capabilities and the need for modernization.

One anon argued that India's military manufacturing capability is not inferior, but it needs to be utilized effectively. Another anon mentioned that the infantry is still stuck in the 1980s due to poor funding.

The thread also included criticism of certain castes, with one anon stating that Dalits are "rapists" and have a "collective IQ of Room Temperature." However, another anon responded, saying that the issue is not with the ideas being discussed but with the repetitive and incoherent way they are presented.

articles.title: Anon inquires about bharatchan's disappearance
articles.body:
Anon asked what happened to bharatchan, why he was called 'gayush dalit', and who owns the board. Anon had been offline for weeks and was seeking information.

Another anon replied that bharatchan had issues with hosting and domain renewals, and was overwhelmed by reports, raids, and internal conflicts, including caste beefs.

A respondent claimed bharatchan was doxxed after mentioning a workplace conversation and his last name, leading to threats against him and his Japanese workplace.

Anon expressed some regret over doxxing bharatchan, and others discussed the circumstances and motivations behind it.

articles.title: Anon Calls for British Colonization of India
articles.body:
Anon posted a tirade against Indians, calling them "shit lovers" and associating the Indian flag with flies.

The poster wished for the British to colonize India again and establish a "pure EUROPEAN AND WESTERN meritocracy." Anon respondents largely shared similar sentiments, with one saying Indians are "retarded" and another calling for a counter-movement against them.

Some discussed cybersecurity and tactics for anonymity online.

The thread also included homophobic and misogynistic comments, with one poster expressing a desire for "fresh kind of pussy." Another suggested that a 50% women's reservation in parliament would be undemocratic.
